name: Cloud Adoption — Standard
description: Pre-populated starter content for Azure cloud adoption and migration engagements following the Microsoft Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF), with workload assessment, landing zone design, and migration waves.

executiveSummary.content:
This engagement guides {{customer_name}} through their Azure cloud adoption journey using Microsoft's Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF). Starting from a structured assessment of the existing on-premises environment, the team will design an Azure Landing Zone, define a migration wave strategy, and execute the migration of agreed workloads — delivering measurable outcomes in scalability, cost optimisation, and operational resilience.

The engagement is structured to de-risk the transition: a thorough Assess & Plan phase validates business cases and surfaces risks before any workload moves, while a pilot wave proves the migration runbook before full-scale execution. Security and compliance controls are embedded throughout, with governance baselines established in the Landing Zone before the first workload migrates.

inScope.text: Cloud Readiness Assessment: application portfolio discovery and Azure suitability scoring
Azure Landing Zone design and deployment (Management Groups, policies, networking, identity)
Migration of agreed workloads across defined migration waves
Azure Cost Management baseline and tagging taxonomy implementation
Security baseline: Microsoft Defender for Cloud enablement, policy assignments, RBAC model
Azure Monitor and Log Analytics workspace configuration for migrated workloads
Operational runbook and Cloud Operations Handbook
Knowledge transfer and Azure Operations training for {{customer_name}} IT team

outOfScope.text: Application modernisation or refactoring (lift-and-shift only unless specified)
Workloads not listed in the agreed migration register
Microsoft Azure consumption costs (billed directly to {{customer_name}}'s Azure subscription)

cloudAdoptionScope.cloudObjectives:
1. Migrate {{workload_count}} agreed workloads to Azure within the engagement timeline
2. Achieve ≥20% reduction in infrastructure TCO within 12 months of migration
3. Establish a fully governed Azure Landing Zone aligned to CAF best practices
4. Enable {{customer_name}} IT team to self-manage Azure operations via documented runbooks and completed training
5. Achieve Defender for Cloud Secure Score ≥70% on migrated workloads at project closure
cloudAdoptionScope.targetEnvironment: Microsoft Azure — {{customer_name}}'s existing Azure tenant (or new tenant provisioned per Landing Zone design). Primary region: {{azure_region}}. Secondary region for DR: {{azure_dr_region}}.

migrationStrategy.approach:
Migration follows Microsoft's CAF Migrate methodology in four stages:

1. Assess: Discover and profile workloads using Azure Migrate; validate Azure suitability, sizing recommendations, and estimated Azure costs per workload.

2. Plan: Define migration waves based on workload dependencies, business priority, and migration complexity. Wave 0 = non-production / pilot workloads.

3. Migrate: Execute lift-and-shift using Azure Migrate (for VMs) and Azure Database Migration Service (for databases). Each wave concludes with a Business Validation checkpoint.

4. Optimise: Post-migration cost review, right-sizing recommendations, and tagging compliance check.

waves.workloads: 2–3 non-production workloads selected for low risk and high learning value
Low-complexity production workloads with no external dependencies
Business-critical systems; requires detailed cutover planning and rollback rehearsal

migrationStrategy.rollbackStrategy: Each wave has a documented rollback procedure. Azure Migrate replicated VMs are kept in warm standby for 72 hours post-cutover. Rollback decision authority rests with {{customer_name}} IT Director. Rollback exercises are conducted during pilot wave.

workloadAssessment.summary: Initial discovery will be performed using Azure Migrate appliance deployed in {{customer_name}}'s on-premises environment. Discovery runs for a minimum of 2 weeks to capture performance data. Output is an Application Portfolio Report with Azure readiness rating (Ready / Ready with conditions / Not ready) and sizing recommendations.

securityCompliance.securityFramework: Security controls are aligned to the Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark (MCSB) and {{customer_name}}'s internal security policy. The Landing Zone establishes baseline Azure Policy assignments (CIS Azure Foundations Benchmark) before any workload migrates.

controls.implementation: Azure AD / Entra ID as identity provider; Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for elevated access; no standing admin access to production subscriptions
Hub-and-Spoke topology with Azure Firewall; NSGs on all subnets; no public IPs on workload VMs without WAF
Microsoft Defender for Cloud (Standard tier) enabled on all subscriptions; alerts routed to Log Analytics workspace
Azure Key Vault for secrets management; encryption at rest and in transit enforced via Azure Policy
Azure Policy compliance dashboard; monthly compliance report shared with {{customer_name}} CISO

securityCompliance.complianceRequirements: {{customer_name}}'s applicable compliance frameworks (e.g. ISO 27001, GDPR, industry-specific) will be mapped to Azure Policy initiatives and reviewed at Landing Zone Design Gate.

assumptions.text: {{customer_name}} will grant Microsoft Contributor access to the target Azure subscription(s) for the duration of the engagement
Azure Migrate appliance can be deployed on-premises and has network visibility to target VMs/databases
Workload owners from {{customer_name}} will be available for validation checkpoints after each migration wave
{{customer_name}} will manage decommissioning of on-premises assets post-migration
Network connectivity between on-premises and Azure (ExpressRoute or Site-to-Site VPN) is in place or provisioned within the engagement timeline

customerResponsibilities.text: Provision target Azure subscriptions and assign Microsoft team the required RBAC roles
Deploy Azure Migrate appliance on-premises and ensure network access to discovery targets
Assign workload owners to validate each wave post-migration
Ensure ExpressRoute or VPN connectivity is available before Wave 0 begins

risks.text: Network connectivity delays blocking migration execution — mitigated by early connectivity validation sprint
Undiscovered workload dependencies causing wave sequencing issues — mitigated by 2-week discovery period and dependency mapping workshop
Azure SQL compatibility issues for database migrations — mitigated by Database Migration Assessment in Assess phase with SQL Server Migration Assistant (SSMA)

members.responsibilities: Schedule, RAID, stakeholder coordination, wave sign-off
Landing Zone design, architecture governance, security baseline
Azure Migrate configuration, VM replication, cutover execution
Database migration assessment, DMS execution, validation
Defender for Cloud, Policy, identity and networking security controls

teamStructure.supportTransitionPlan: Post-migration: 2-week hypercare per wave (Microsoft on-call for P1/P2). At project closure: Cloud Operations Handbook delivered, Azure training completed with {{customer_name}} IT team, and Microsoft support contacts provided.

supportOperations.operationsModel: {{customer_name}} IT team takes operational ownership of migrated workloads at project closure. Microsoft delivers a Cloud Operations Handbook covering: monitoring runbooks, cost management procedures, security patching cadence, backup verification, and incident escalation paths.
supportOperations.monitoringSetup: Azure Monitor + Log Analytics workspace configured with: VM performance alerts (CPU >85%, Memory >90%), availability alerts, and cost anomaly alerts. Dashboards delivered in Azure Monitor Workbooks.
supportOperations.supportContacts: Post-engagement support via Microsoft's standard support channels. Recommended: {{customer_name}} to hold a Microsoft Azure support plan (Developer or above) for ongoing cloud operations.
